description: When the evaluating engine itself restarts mid-hold, the in-progress for:-hold does not survive and is not reconstructed from persisted elapsed time; once the engine resumes and the underlying condition freshly re-matches, the hold counts again from zero, and its interruption is never recorded as a misfire.

events.note: hold timer starts, counting toward the 300s for:
engine restarts with 120000ms of the hold already elapsed
the durable state is unchanged; per RUL-025/RUL-300 this is not itself a new transition

results.reason: no in-flight run or timer state survives an engine restart

the post-restart observation reconfirms the durable unreachable state rather than producing a fresh transition into it (RUL-025/RUL-300), so the hold does not begin counting again here; it will begin only on a genuine fresh transition into unreachable
RUL-362: an interrupted in-progress hold never itself carries the misfire_caught marker — it is a distinct mechanism from misfire policy
